rationale.summary: Vote: ABSTAIN. For this InfoAction (expired), evidence quality was insufficient for a reliable YES or NO decision. Primary blocker: DRep distribution signal was unavailable for this action. Decisive reason: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The K parameter was created to limit the total number of ADA staked to one pool." and clears the evidence gates. What would enable a directional vote: Provide mitigation evidence; Provide independent assurance. Residual uncertainty: No DRep distribution available. Confidence context: score -0.05; confidence 58.2%.
rationale.summary_raw: Advisory model lean was +0.030 (display cap ±0.05) and had zero influence on the binding score: Doctrine-aware offline lean for a info action: 8 well-supported claim(s). Net bounded adjustment +0.030 (clamped to ±0.05).

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: The K parameter was created to limit the total number of ADA staked to one pool., The K parameter determines the saturation of stakepools., To calculate the saturation of stakepools you can take the total number of ADA and divide it by K., K is currently 500, and pool saturation is currently 74,551,771.86 ADA.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The K parameter was created to limit the total number of ADA staked to one pool.
Claim (other, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The K parameter determines the saturation of stakepools.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): To calculate the saturation of stakepools you can take the total number of ADA and divide it by K.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): K is currently 500, and pool saturation is currently 74,551,771.86 ADA.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): This governance action is by no means a suggestion of what Cardano protocol parameters should be modified to or if they should be modified at this time.
Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The goal of this governance action is to increase governance participation before the next hardfork.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The K parameter was created to limit the total number of ADA staked to one pool." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: weak controls, unclear delivery, or governance precedent could outweigh the benefit of InfoAction.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.
